title: 9 Herbal Creams For Bad Breath
intro_desc: Herbal creams for bad breath are a natural way to tackle this issue. They work by using the antibacterial properties of certain herbs to kill the bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. One example of this is Eucalyptus globulus, which has been shown to reduce the amount of bacteria in your mouth and help to freshen your breath.  Eucalyptus globulus has decongestant properties, which help to clear out mucus from your sinuses, reducing inflammation and helping to prevent the build-up of bacteria that cause bad breath. Another herb that is often used in these creams is Rosmarinus officinalis. This herb has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which help to kill bacteria and fungi that can cause bad breath.  Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, is also often used in herbal creams for bad breath. It has a cooling effect on the mouth and throat, which helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. The antibacterial properties of these herbs help to prevent the build-up of bacteria that can cause bad breath, and can even help to prevent gum disease and other oral health issues.  Using herbal creams for bad breath can have many benefits for your life. For one thing, it's a natural way to tackle bad breath, without resorting to harsh chemicals or artificial fragrances. It's also a more targeted approach to oral health, focusing on the specific causes of bad breath rather than just masking the symptoms. This can be especially beneficial for people who experience bad breath due to a specific medical condition or oral health issue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains compounds like eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, which have antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is a common cause of bad breath. Eucalyptol, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a type of bacteria that contributes to bad breath and tooth decay. The antiseptic properties of alpha-pinene also help to kill bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth, reducing the likelihood of bad breath. By reducing the presence of bacteria and other microorganisms, Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to eliminate bad breath and promote a healthy mouth environment.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 10 drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add distilled water and stir well.
Add Eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into small containers or jars.
Let the mixture cool and solidify completely. Once solid, your Eucalyptus globulus cream for bad breath is ready to use. Apply a small amount to your tongue or gums as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that fights bacteria in the mouth. This acid has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of germs that cause bad breath. The cream also contains carnosic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gums and reduce swelling. The camphor and borneol in Rosmarinus officinalis creams have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help eliminate bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth. By reducing bacteria and inflammation, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can help freshen breath and prevent bad breath.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in rosemary essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify, then use as needed to help freshen breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains active constituents like menthol and menthone. These compounds have antibacterial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Menthol also has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the gums and mouth. Additionally, menthone has antiseptic properties, which can help to kill bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to bad breath. By using these properties, Mentha x piperita creams can help to freshen breath and improve oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of dried Mentha x piperita (peppermint) leaves, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until fully melted.
Steep the dried Mentha x piperita leaves in 2 tablespoons of boiling water for 5 minutes. Let it cool, then strain the liquid and discard the solids.
Add the peppermint liquid, vitamin E oil, and the melted coconut oil-beeswax mixture to a blender or food processor. Blend until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or ice cube trays. Let it cool and harden before use. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, tannins, and ursolic acid as its active constituents. These compounds have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Rosmarinic acid also has antioxidant properties that protect the mouth from damage and promote a healthy environment. The anti-inflammatory properties of ursolic acid help soothe and reduce inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to bad breath. By reducing bacterial growth and inflammation, Salvia officinalis creams can help alleviate bad breath and maintain a healthy mouth.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until it reaches 180°F.
Add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves to the heated coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
In a separate bowl, melt the beeswax in the microwave or in the double boiler. Add the melted beeswax to the Salvia-infused coconut oil and stir until combined.
Add the vitamin E oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into small tin containers or jars and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active constituents that help combat bad breath. Thymol has antimicrobial properties, which kill bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath. Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and tongue, common causes of bad breath. Thymus vulgaris creams also contain eugenol, which acts as an antioxidant and helps protect the mouth from damage caused by free radicals. The combination of these properties in Thymus vulgaris creams makes it effective in reducing bad breath and promoting oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the dried Thymus vulgaris leaves to the melted oil and wax, and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container, let it solidify, and use as a bad breath remedy by applying a small amount to your gums and tongue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active compounds like gingerols and shogaols. These compounds have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Gingerols and shogaols also have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. The antimicrobial properties of Zingiber officinale creams help kill the bacteria that cause bad breath, while its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the mouth and gums. By reducing bacteria and inflammation, Zingiber officinale creams can help freshen breath and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of ginger essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler. Stir until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and add ginger essential oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once solid, your Zingiber officinale cream for bad breath is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains bioactive constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate. These compounds have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath. The anti-inflammatory properties of linalool and linalyl acetate in Lavandula angustifolia creams can soothe and calm the gums, reducing swelling and irritation that can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of these compounds can help kill bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth, further reducing bad breath. Regular use of Lavandula angustifolia creams can promote a healthy oral environment and fresh breath.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments until melted.
Add shea butter and stir until melted and well combined.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly, then add lavender essential oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams contains compounds like limonene, geraniol, and linalool. These active constituents have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. The antimicrobial properties of these compounds also help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can lead to gum disease and bad breath. Furthermore, the antiseptic properties of Cymbopogon citratus creams help to kill the bacteria in the mouth and on the tongue that cause bad breath. By reducing the bacteria in the mouth, Cymbopogon citratus creams helps to leave the breath smelling fresh and clean.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat.
Add 10-15 fresh leaves of Cymbopogon citratus (also known as lemon grass) to the saucepan. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the cooled mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, use the Cymbopogon citratus cream as needed to freshen bad breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare creams contains carvacrol, a potent antioxidant that helps kill bacteria causing bad breath. This oil also has thymol, a compound that reduces inflammation in the mouth and throat. The antimicrobial properties of carvacrol and thymol in Origanum vulgare creams help eliminate the bacteria that cause bad breath. The antiseptic properties of Origanum vulgare creams reduce plaque and prevent gum disease, further contributing to a fresher breath. Regular use of Origanum vulgare creams can lead to a decrease in volatile sulfur compounds, which are responsible for bad breath.
remedies.image_alt: Origanum vulgare herbal creams for bad breath
remedy_procedure: Gather 1/2 cup of dried Origanum vulgare leaves, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the dried Origanum vulgare leaves to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a tin or a jar and let it cool and solidify before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent bad breath from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and eucalyptus, have antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. These teas can also help reduce inflammation and freshen your breath, making them a natural way to keep your mouth feeling clean.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good option for bad breath, but consuming them every day might not be the best idea. Some herbs can be too strong, and overdoing it can lead to stomach issues or interact with other medications. It's best to use them occasionally to freshen your breath naturally.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with bad breath. The results may vary, but some people notice a difference within a few days to a week. As you drink the tea regularly, you might start to feel fresher in your mouth and notice a reduction in bad breath.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for bad breath can be most effective in the morning, when your body is waking up and your digestive system is starting to work. This is because many herbal teas, such as peppermint and chamomile, have antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria causing bad breath.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas might interact with prescription medications. For example, if you take blood thinners, drinking teas like ginkgo or garlic might make them less effective. This could be a problem for people with bad breath who also take medications like antibiotics or antacids. Herbal teas can affect how these medications work.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like sage and peppermint, are commonly used for bad breath, but their safety during pregnancy isn't fully known. To be safe, choose teas that are known to be safe for pregnant women, like ginger or chamomile, and always follow the recommended amounts.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful remedy for bad breath, but it's essential to choose ones that are safe for breastfeeding. Look for teas made from ingredients like peppermint, chamomile, or licorice root, which are generally considered okay. However, teas containing sage or eucalyptus may not be the best choice, as they can pass into breast milk and affect your baby.
title_2: 9 Herbal Creams for Gum Disease Sufferers with Bad Breath
